title: Incorrect Longest Increasing Path in Matrix

statement.markdown: Given a square matrix `matrix` where each element is a non-negative integer, find the **longest increasing path** from any cell to any cell diagonally or horizontally/vertically next to it. The path must be monotonically increasing, i.e., the value of the next cell in the path must be greater than or equal to the current cell.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a 2D list of integers `matrix` representing the square matrix.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the maximum length of the longest increasing path in the matrix.

hints.text: To find the maximum increasing path, consider each cell as the starting point.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Use a depth-first search approach to traverse the matrix and find the maximum increasing path.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Try all possible paths from each cell.
editorial.betterSolution: Use DFS with memoization to store the maximum increasing path length for each cell.
editorial.optimalSolution: Sort each row in the matrix in descending order and then perform a level order traversal to find the maximum increasing path.

starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int longestIncreasingPath(int[][] matrix) { /* TODO: implement */ } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def longestIncreasingPath(self, matrix: List[List[int]]) -> int: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int longestIncreasingPath(vector<vector<int>>& matrix) { int n = matrix.size(); int res = INT_MIN; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) { int count = 0; if (i > 0 && matrix[i][j] > matrix[i-1][j]) count++; if (j > 0 && matrix[i][j] > matrix[i][j-1]) count++; res = max(res, count); } } return res; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int longestIncreasingPath(int[][] matrix) { int n = matrix.length; int res = Integer.MIN_VALUE; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) { int count = 0; if (i > 0 && matrix[i][j] > matrix[i-1][j]) count++; if (j > 0 && matrix[i][j] > matrix[i][j-1]) count++; res = Math.max(res, count); } } return res; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def longestIncreasingPath(self, matrix: List[List[int]]) -> int: n = len(matrix); res = 1; for i in range(n): for j in range(n): count = 0; if i > 0 and matrix[i][j] > matrix[i-1][j]: count++; if j > 0 and matrix[i][j] > matrix[i][j-1]: count++; res = max(res, count); return res;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function longestIncreasingPath(matrix) { let n = matrix.length; let res = 0; for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) { for (let j = 0; j < n; j++) { let count = 0; if (i > 0 && matrix[i][j] > matrix[i-1][j]) count++; if (j > 0 && matrix[i][j] > matrix[i][j-1]) count++; res = Math.max(res, count); } } return res; }
